pattern: A split point can be dropped in front of any position, and only the first character of each piece is ever constrained, so the true limit on how many pieces you can make is simply how many distinct starting letters the whole string has to offer.
hints: Nothing about a substring except its very first character is constrained, so try to imagine cutting the string the instant you meet a letter you have not used to start a piece with yet.
You are always free to end the current piece right before any position, no matter how short or long it has become, as long as the next piece begins with an unused letter.

Because a cut can be made in front of the very first occurrence of any character, the maximum number of pieces you can ever reach equals the number of distinct letters that appear anywhere in the string.

edge_inputs.description: statement example, two distinct letters alternating
statement example, only one distinct letter
a repeated letter separates two other distinct letters
ten distinct letters each appear exactly once
the string revisits every letter many times after they first appear

stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100000))
    alphabet = 'abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz'
    s = ''.join(rng.choice(alphabet) for _ in range(n))
    return [s]
